reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via P2P beaconDataColumnsByRoot by sending DataColumnSidecar with column.len != kzg_commitments.len. The sync path (request_manager.nim:176-194) validates only the inclusion proof via verify_data_column_sidecar_inclusion_proof() but skips verify_data_column_sidecar(), which contains the length equality check (peerdas_helpers.nim:362-364). The malformed sidecar is added to quarantine (L437), then block_processor.nim:687 calls verify_data_column_sidecar_kzg_proofs() which builds cellIndices array with column.len but passes mismatched-length arrays to the C library verifyCellKzgProofBatch(). The gossip path correctly calls verify_data_column_sidecar() which would reject this, but the sync path bypasses it. Spec reference: 01e_PARTIAL_W3B4_1771748827.json line 78-93 requires 'All input list lengths must be equal before batch verification proceeds.' Severity remains Medium as the impact is limited to a single client crash rather than network-wide consensus failure.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: All input list lengths must be equal before batch verification proceeds. (PROP-57888860-pre-002 from 01e_PARTIAL_W3B4_1771748827.json)
